accurate.why: All claims match the bill text; reporting requirements and timelines are correctly described.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated content; all specific provisions cited match the bill.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the Act as requiring FEMA to report and review disaster assistance for people with disabilities.

changes_to_existing_law.why: States the Act 'does not amend' existing law, but the bill does require a report pursuant to major disaster declarations under the Stafford Act; the overall claim is accurate.

exceptions_conditions.why: No material exceptions or conditions apply in this bill.

effective_dates.why: Correctly notes the 120-day and one-year deadlines.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers both the 120-day FEMA report and the one-year GAO/FEMA review requirements with their specific content requirements.

coherent.why: Well-organized with clear delineation of the two main requirements.

concise.why: Quite lengthy, repeating sub-requirements for both the report and the review that could be condensed.

correct_entities.why: FEMA Administrator, Comptroller General, House Transportation and Infrastructure Committee, Senate Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs Committee all correctly identified.

correct_figures.why: 120-day and one-year deadlines match the bill; November 25, 2024 enactment date is accurate.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
